89.content: Yes, I've been here since 1976.

89.speaker: NIGEL
89.content: Then why do you want to sell it?

89.speaker: IAN
89.content: Because I've just retired.

89.speaker: IAN
89.content: I want to buy a small house in the country.

89.speaker: NIGEL
89.content: How much does this house cost?

89.speaker: NIGEL
89.content: That's a lot of money!

89.speaker: IAN
89.content: It's worth every penny of it.

89.speaker: NIGEL
89.content: Well, I like the house, but I can't decide yet.

89.speaker: NIGEL
89.content: My wife must see it first.

89.speaker: IAN
89.content: Women always have the last word.

93.content: Nigel is our new next-door neighbour.
The month after next he'll fly to Tokyo.
At the moment, he's in Madrid.
He flew to Spain a week ago.
He'll return to London the week after next.
He's only forty-one years old, and he has already been to nearly every country in the world.
Nigel is a very lucky man.
But his wife isn't very lucky.

101.speaker: PENNY

103.speaker: GARY
103.content: How was the exam, Richard?

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: Not too bad.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: I think I passed in English and Mathematics.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: The questions were very easy.

103.content: How about you, Gary?

103.speaker: GARY
103.content: I hope I haven't failed.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: I think I failed the French paper.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: I could answer sixteen of the questions.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: They were very easy.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: But I couldn't answer the rest.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: They were too difficult for me.

103.speaker: GARY
103.content: French tests are awful, aren't they?

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: I hate them.

103.speaker: RICHARD
103.content: I'm sure I've got a low mark.

103.speaker: GARY
103.content: Oh, cheer up!

103.speaker: GARY
103.content: Perhaps we didn't do too badly.

103.speaker: GARY
103.content: The guy next to me wrote his name at the top of the paper.

103.speaker: GARY
103.content: Then he sat there and looked at it for three hours!

103.speaker: GARY

117.content: When my husband was going into the dining room this morning, he dropped some coins on the floor.
We looked for them, but we could not find them all.
While we were having breakfast, our little boy, Tommy, found two small coins on thefloor.
He put them both into his mouth.
We both tried to get the coins.
Later that morning, when I was doing the housework, my husband phoned me from the office.
"I don't know,' I answered, 'Tommy's been to the toilet three times this morning.
but I haven't had any change yet!'

119.content: I want to tell you a true story.
It happened to a friend of mine a year ago.
While my friend, George, was reading in bed, two thieves climbed into his kitchen.
After they had entered the house, they went into the dining room.
It was very dark, so they turned on a torch Suddenly, they heard a voice behind them.
The thieves dropped the torch and ran away as quickly as they could.
George heard the noise and came downstairs quickly.
He turned on the light, but he couldn't see anyone.
But George's parrot, Henry, was still there.

120.content: I asked the price of the car, I ran to the platform quickly, but they had already sold it.
but the train had already left.

138.content: BOOK If you break this window, If you don't hurry, you'll have to pay for it!
If he falls, If it rains tomorrow, he'll hurt himself.
If you feel better, If he sells that car, you can get up.
he can buy a new one.

139.content: Which John Smith does Graham Turner think he is talking to?

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: Tell Mary we'll be late for dinner this evening.

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: She invited Charlotte and me to dinner this evening.

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: I said I would be at your house at six o'clock, but the boss wants me to do some extra work.

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: I'll have to stay at the office.

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: I don't know when I'll finish.

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: Oh, and by the way, my wife wants to know if Mary needs any help.

139.speaker: JOHN SMITH
139.content: I don't know what you're talking about.

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: That is John Smith, isn't it?

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: You are John Smith, the engineer, aren't you?

139.speaker: GRAHAM TURNER
139.content: You Work for the Overseas Engineering Company, don't you?

139.speaker: JOHN SMITH
139.content: I'm John Smith the telephone engineer and I'm repairing your telephone line.

141.content: Last week, my four-year-old daughter, Sally.
was invited to a children's party.
I decided to take her by train.
Sally was very excited because she had never travelled on a train before.
She sat near the window and asked questions about everything she saw.
Suddenly, a middle-aged lady got on the train and sat opposite Sally.
Sally did not answer, but looked at her curiously.
The lady was dressed in a blue coat and a large, funny hat.
After the train had left the station, She then began to make up her face.
"Why are you doing that?' Sally asked.
'To make myself beautiful,' the lady answered.
She put away her compact and smiled kindly.
'But you are still ugly,' Sally said.
Sally was amused, but I was very embarrassed!

143.content: I live in a very old town which is surrounded by beautiful woods.
It is a famous beauty spot.
On Sundays, hundreds of people come from the city to see our town and to walk through the woods.
Visitors have been asked to keep the woods clean and tidy.
Litter baskets have been placed under the trees.
but people still throw their rubbish everywhere.
Last Wednesday, I went for a walk in the woods.
What I saw made me very sad.
I counted seven old cars and three old refrigerators.
The litter baskets were empty and the ground was covered with pieces of paper, cigarette ends, old tyres, empty bottles and rusty tins.
Among the rubbish, I found a sign which said, Anyone who leaves litter in these woods will be prosecuted !'
